subject: Product Management
title: Re-finding Fit
description: A tiny lesson with a big payoff: Re-finding Fit.

hook.text: Some companies lose PMF and claw it back. Marvel, LEGO, and Apple all fell from grace and returned stronger.

buildup.text: Apple nearly went bankrupt in 1997. Steve Jobs returned and killed 70% of the product line to focus on four products.

discovery.text: LEGO was losing $1 million per day in 2003. New leadership refocused on the core brick and licensed franchises.

twist.text: In every comeback story, the pattern is the same: ruthless focus on what made the product special in the first place.

climax.text: Re-finding PMF requires honesty about what changed and courage to cut everything that no longer serves the core value.

punchline.text: To refind fit, cut everything that isn't the core.

quiz.question: What pattern do PMF comeback stories share?
options: Ruthless focus on the core value — cutting everything that doesn't serve it
Adding more features to attract new markets
Lowering prices to win back customers
